skill.name: pathml
skill.description: PathML guidance for computational pathology workflows, including whole-slide image loading, preprocessing, graph construction, multiparametric imaging, data management, and machine learning.
skill.summary: Guide PathML workflows for whole-slide image analysis and spatial pathology.
skill.author: davila7

security_audit.summary: AI review found no prompt injection and no executable skill hooks; the critical heuristic finding is dismissed as a false positive caused by documentation examples. The skill is medium risk because its legitimate PathML workflows include package installation, local file reads and writes, shell job-script examples, remote DeepCell API examples, and PyTorch checkpoint loading that require trusted inputs and user consent.

medium_findings.title: Remote Biomedical Image Processing Example
medium_findings.description: The references document SegmentMIFRemote with DeepCell API URLs. This is legitimate PathML functionality, but users could send sensitive biomedical image channels to an external service without explicit data-governance review.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The API endpoints and remote inference context are explicit. The risk depends on user data sensitivity, so this is a clear workflow concern rather than confirmed malicious behavior.

medium_findings.title: Batch Script Example Uses Unquoted Slide Path
medium_findings.description: The data-management reference generates a SLURM script that reads slide paths and runs a Python command with an unquoted shell variable. If copied directly and used with attacker-controlled file names, this could cause command injection or processing of unintended files.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The shell snippet and unquoted variable are visible, but it is documentation rather than executable skill code. Exploitability depends on users copying it and processing untrusted paths.

medium_findings.title: PyTorch Checkpoint Loading Requires Trusted Files
medium_findings.description: The feature extraction example loads a local PyTorch checkpoint. PyTorch checkpoint loading can execute unsafe serialized content when the model file is untrusted, so generated workflows should require trusted checkpoint sources.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The code directly loads a checkpoint path in a documented example. The risk is real for untrusted checkpoints, though the example uses a local project path and shows no malicious intent.

low_findings.title: Static Analyzer Markdown False Positives
low_findings.description: Most high and medium static hits are fenced Markdown examples or inline code references, not executable skill hooks. Examples include PathML imports, model.eval() evaluation mode calls, and resource URLs.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review confirmed these locations are documentation examples or reference links. No prompt injection text, hidden executable file, or automatic command runner was found.

dangerous_patterns.title: Remote API Endpoint in Workflow Example
dangerous_patterns.description: The documentation shows remote DeepCell inference endpoints. This pattern can expose biomedical image data if used without approval.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The remote API configuration is explicit in the examples. It is a legitimate feature, but it has privacy implications for sensitive image data.

dangerous_patterns.title: Generated Shell Job Script
dangerous_patterns.description: The documentation creates a shell script for SLURM batch processing and interpolates a slide path into a Python command. This should be hardened before use with untrusted paths.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The shell construction is present and can be risky in copied workflows. It is not automatically executed by the skill.

dangerous_patterns.title: PyTorch Checkpoint Deserialization
dangerous_patterns.description: The documentation loads a model checkpoint with torch.load. Users should only load checkpoints from trusted sources.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The example clearly calls torch.load on a checkpoint path. The security impact depends on checkpoint provenance.

content.value_statement: Whole-slide pathology workflows are complex and depend on specialized formats, preprocessing, and model steps. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code practical PathML guidance for reproducible image analysis.
actual_capabilities: Explains how to load WSI formats such as SVS, NDPI, DICOM, and OME-TIFF with PathML.
Guides H&E preprocessing with tissue detection, stain normalization, artifact labeling, and tiling workflows.
Describes graph construction for cell, tissue, and spatial transcriptomics analysis.
Covers HoVer-Net, HACTNet, model evaluation, ONNX export, and PyTorch-based pathology workflows.
Supports CODEX, Vectra, MERFISH, and multiplex immunofluorescence analysis patterns.
Documents HDF5 storage, batch processing, feature extraction, and dataset organization for large pathology projects.
limitations: The skill provides guidance and examples; it does not include a runnable PathML package or datasets.
Users must install PathML and required native imaging dependencies in their own environment.
Medical outputs require expert review and are not clinical diagnostic advice.
Remote segmentation examples can send image data to an external service if users choose that workflow.

use_cases.title: Prepare WSI Analysis Pipelines
use_cases.description: Plan loading, tiling, quality control, stain normalization, and HDF5 storage for large slide collections.

use_cases.title: Build Spatial Biology Workflows
use_cases.description: Use CODEX, Vectra, and MERFISH guidance to segment cells, quantify markers, and prepare spatial analysis outputs.

use_cases.title: Prototype Pathology ML Models
use_cases.description: Adapt HoVer-Net, HACTNet, graph models, and evaluation patterns for segmentation and classification experiments.

prompt_templates.title: Load a Slide
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner setup for a single whole-slide image.
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me load a whole-slide image with PathML. Ask for the file format, desired resolution, and whether I need tiles or metadata first.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Preprocessing Plan
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a PathML preprocessing pipeline for H&E slides with tissue detection, stain normalization, artifact checks, and HDF5 output. Include safety checks before writing files.

prompt_templates.title: Analyze Multiplex Imaging
prompt_templates.prompt: Plan a PathML workflow for CODEX or Vectra data. Include channel handling, cell segmentation, marker quantification, and export options for single-cell analysis.

prompt_templates.title: Review an ML Experiment
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced model training and evaluation review.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review my PathML machine learning plan for nucleus segmentation. Check data splits, preprocessing, model loading safety, metrics, and deployment risks.

output_examples.input: I need to process 500 SVS files for tissue detection and storage.
output_examples.output: A staged workflow covering dependency checks, slide inventory, tile sizing, tissue masks, HDF5 output naming, batch execution, and validation steps.

output_examples.input: I want to quantify markers from CODEX images.
output_examples.output: A channel-aware analysis plan with segmentation options, marker measurement steps, quality control checks, and export guidance for downstream spatial analysis.

output_examples.input: I need to evaluate a HoVer-Net model for nuclei segmentation.
output_examples.output: A review checklist for trusted checkpoints, held-out datasets, Dice, AJI, panoptic quality, inference export, and result inspection.

best_practices: Confirm slide formats, metadata, and pyramid levels before building a full batch workflow.
Use explicit output directories and review file writes before processing large datasets.
Use trusted model checkpoints and get consent before remote API processing of biomedical images.
anti_patterns: Do not run batch commands over clinical data without validating paths, permissions, and output locations.
Do not send protected or sensitive image data to remote services without approval.
Do not load untrusted PyTorch checkpoint files or treat model output as a diagnosis.

faq.question: Does this skill include PathML itself?
faq.answer: No. It provides PathML workflow guidance and references. Users must install PathML separately.

faq.question: Can it work with whole-slide image formats?
faq.answer: Yes. The references cover formats such as SVS, NDPI, DICOM, OME-TIFF, and other microscopy formats.

faq.question: Can it help with multiplex immunofluorescence?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes guidance for CODEX, Vectra, MERFISH, segmentation, and marker quantification workflows.

faq.question: Is it safe for clinical diagnosis?
faq.answer: No. It is for research and engineering workflows. Clinical use requires validated systems and expert review.

faq.question: Why is there a security warning?
faq.answer: The documentation includes examples for local file access, package installation, remote APIs, shell job scripts, and model loading.

faq.question: How should sensitive pathology data be handled?
faq.answer: Keep data local when required, review outputs before writing, avoid unapproved remote APIs, and follow institutional privacy rules.
